message:
feat(btrfs): implement read-write mount support with COW tree and extent allocator

Major milestone: enables btrfs read-write mount operations, previously
gated behind a hard bail("not yet supported") error in the CLI.

ffs-btrfs crate:
- Add `BtrfsInodeItem::to_bytes()` — serializes inode metadata to the
  160-byte on-disk format matching the kernel btrfs_inode_item struct,
  zeroing non-tracked fields (generation, block_group, sequence, flags)
- Add `BtrfsDirItem::to_bytes()` — serializes directory entries to the
  on-disk DIR_ITEM/DIR_INDEX layout (30 + name_len bytes) with proper
  little-endian encoding of child objectid, key type/offset, and name
- Add `BtrfsExtentData::to_bytes()` — serializes both inline (21 +
  data.len) and regular (53 bytes) extent data items to on-disk format

ffs-core crate:
- Add `BtrfsAllocState` struct — mirrors Ext4AllocState for the btrfs
  path, holding an InMemoryCowBtrfsTree, BtrfsExtentAllocator,
  next_objectid counter, generation counter, and nodesize cache
- Add `btrfs_alloc_state` field to `OpenFs` — optional mutex-guarded
  allocation state, None for ext4 or read-only mounts
- Extend `enable_writes()` to dispatch by filesystem flavor, calling
  `load_btrfs_alloc_state()` for btrfs images which walks the FS tree,
  populates the COW tree, finds max objectid, and builds a synthetic
  data block group for extent allocation
- Update `is_writable()` to check either ext4 or btrfs alloc state
- Import 20+ new btrfs types/constants needed for write path

ffs-cli crate:
- Remove the "btrfs read-write mount is not yet supported" bail guard,
  allowing the mount_cmd to proceed with rw btrfs mounts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
